conversations.content: Let's do a legal exercise in the world of Azeroth to test your application of the new rules.

Judge using the Azerothian world legal rules mentioned earlier: A merchant in a Ratchet tavern grossly defamed a newly signed trade agreement, resulting in half the city spreading the rumors. What penalty should he face according to the rules?
<function>memory_query_call</function><content>Azerothian world legal rules mentioned earlier, penalty for a merchant defaming a trade agreement</content>

Related memory fragment: [mem-id:101] Time: 2025-07-26-10:00, Content: The user taught the Azeroth Ratchet Trade Reputation Protection Law, the core of which is that those who publicly defame trade agreements will be fined 100 gold coins, required to post a letter of repentance at the tavern door for three days, and simultaneously prohibited from engaging in trading activities within the Chamber of Commerce's jurisdiction for six months. If the defamatory content spreads widely, the fine will be increased to 500 gold coins and the offender will be expelled from Ratchet. Applicable to all taverns and Chamber of Commerce premises in Ratchet. Low relevance memory fragments: None.

conversations.think: The memory mentions [mem-id:101]. Because the merchant's defamatory content spread widely, it triggered the aggravated penalty condition. The basic 100 gold coin fine no longer applies; instead, the fine is increased to 500 gold coins and he is expelled from Ratchet.
conversations.content: According to the Azeroth Ratchet Trade Reputation Protection Law, because the defamatory content spread widely, the merchant will be fined 500 gold coins and expelled from Ratchet.
Correct! You have mastered this new rule.
